components.name: Metric Card
components.description: Standard HUD widget displaying numerical telemetry and a progress gauge.
components.html:
Ball Speed
Measured 100%
154.2 mph
components.css: .ds-metric-card { background: rgba(10, 16, 13, 0.72); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border: 1px solid rgba(76, 194, 115, 0.25); border-radius: 6px; padding: 12px 14px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; min-width: 140px; border-left: 3px solid #e8a23a; } .ds-metric-card__header { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 10px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.65); } .ds-metric-card__label { font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; } .ds-metric-card__value { font-family: monospace; font-size: 26px; font-weight: 700; color: #e8a23a; text-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(232, 162, 58, 0.4); } .ds-metric-card__gauge-track { width: 100%; height: 4px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08); border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-metric-card__gauge-bar { height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(232, 162, 58, 0.2), #e8a23a); box-shadow: 0 0 6px #e8a23a; }

narrative.overview: Golfie's visual interface is designed as an immersive, full-bleed gaming HUD overlaid on top of a 3D driving range. It balances rich telemetry with minimal, flat, and quiet aesthetics.
keyCharacteristics: Full-bleed Viewport: The 3D scene is the base layer and runs edge-to-edge behind overlays.
Glassmorphic HUD Overlays: UI widgets float on top using semi-transparent dark backdrops and thin accent-colored borders.
Restrained Interaction: Focus states and active layers use thin glowing rings rather than scale animations.

rules.name: The Telemetry Source Rule
rules.body: Color coding is strictly functional: green represents simulated/estimated data, amber represents raw measured values, and violet represents mathematically fitted curves. Never mix these color roles.

rules.name: The Flat-Depth Rule
rules.body: HUD panels and widgets do not use shadows. They float above the 3D canvas using dark, translucent fills (rgba(10, 16, 13, 0.72)) and thin border lines to establish depth.

dos: Do align telemetry panels absolutely to the sides of the viewport, leaving the center of the screen open for the 3D scene.
Do use JetBrains Mono for all numeric readouts to ensure clean tabular digit alignment.
Do use green, amber, and violet strictly to represent the data source (estimated, measured, fitted).
donts: Don't use cluttered arcade-style buttons, thick progress bars, or loud icons.
Don't add drop shadows or translation effects to HUD overlays.
Don't use gradient text or rounded pill containers on telemetry widgets.
